The scent of berbere and roasted coffee mingling with the damp, metallic smell of the Fillmore rain
The floorboards beneath my pedals hum in a low E-flat as the 22-Fillmore bus rumbles past
The last chord—a bruised B-flat major—hanging in the humid air, refusing to dissipate
I feel the vibration of a passing truck travel up my ulna through a flat fifth
The vibration of the heavy haul truck humming through the floorboards and deep into my teeth
